originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
content: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

title: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists
content: Judges handed down sentences ranging from five years to life imprisonment following mass trials.

title: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
content: UK government officials say they are not entirely abandoning the agreement but have run out of time.

title: Iranian authorities remain defiant, urge supporters to stay in streets
content: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who led Iran's delegation in talks to end the war, said US delegation 'failed to gain trust'.

title: Seven ways America can win the ceasefire and end the war
content: Trump needs to take decisive action to secure regional peace and a win in the midterm elections.

title: Video: Severe flooding closes schools in Saudi Arabia
content: Saudi Arabia has ordered schools to close after severe flooding swept through parts of the country.

originalTitle: After Iran talks falter, the big question is what happens next?
title: After Iran talks falter, the big question is what happens next?
content: Twenty-one hours was not enough to end 47 years of hostility between Iran and the US, writes the BBC's Lyse Doucet.

title: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
content: Her infectious voice got fans dancing and singing, becoming the soundtrack for generations of Indians.

title: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

originalTitle: Wrexham not down and out yet - Parkinson
title: Wrexham not down and out yet - Parkinson
content: Phil Parkinson says his side are still in the mix to earn a Championship play-off place, despite their blunt showing in a 2-0 loss to Birmingham City.

originalTitle: What is going on with Garnacho?
title: What is going on with Garnacho?
content: Alejandro Garnacho is struggling to stand out at Chelsea - so can the £40m signing from Manchester United still realise his potential at Stamford Bridge?

originalTitle: Union Berlin appoint first female boss in Europe's top men's leagues
title: Amazing News! A Woman Leads a Big Football Team!
content:
Hello, Future Football Stars!

Hello, amazing kids! It's your friendly news reporter for Africa here! I have some super exciting news from the world of football. Football is that fun game where you kick a ball, right? Lots of you love to play football. And lots of you love to watch football! Well, something really special just happened in a far-away country called Germany. It's a country in Europe. Are you ready for the big news? Let's go!

Meet Marie-Louise Eta!

We are going to talk about a wonderful lady named Marie-Louise Eta. Can you say that name? Marie-Louise Eta! She is a very smart and kind lady. And guess what? She loves football, just like you! She has played football for a long time. She is very good at football. Now, she is doing something super important. She is helping a big football team. This team plays with men. And Marie-Louise Eta is the one telling them what to do! Marie-Louise Eta has made history! History means something that has never happened before. And this is truly something new. She is the first woman ever to do this job in a very, very big football competition. This is big news for everyone, especially for girls who love sports.

What Does a Football Coach Do?

Do you know what a football coach does? A coach is like a special teacher for a sports team. The coach helps the players get better. They teach them how to kick the ball. They teach them how to pass the ball. They teach them how to score goals. A coach also tells the players where to stand on the field. They make a plan for the game. They help the team win! Marie-Louise Eta is now this special teacher for a men's football team. She is the boss on the field! She will help the team play their very best. It's a very big job! Did you know? There are many different jobs in football! Not just players. There are coaches, referees, doctors, and even people who take care of the grass! All these jobs help make the game wonderful.

Union Berlin: A Football Team!

The football team that Marie-Louise Eta is helping is called Union Berlin. Can you say "Union Berlin"? Union Berlin is a football club. It's a very old and famous club from Germany. They have many, many fans. Fans are people who love the team and cheer for them. Union Berlin plays in a very big stadium. Lots of people come to watch their games. The team wears special uniforms. They train very hard every day to be the best. And now, they have a new helper, Marie-Louise Eta! She will help them play even better. She will help them win games. It's a team full of great players, and now they have a great leader in Marie-Louise Eta.

The Bundesliga: A Very Big Football League!

Union Berlin plays in something called the Bundesliga. What is the Bundesliga? It's like a super big competition! Imagine a big league where all the best football teams in Germany play against each other. That's the Bundesliga! It's one of the best football leagues in the whole world. Lots of people watch these games on TV. Only the very best teams get to play in the Bundesliga. And now, a woman, Marie-Louise Eta, is helping to lead one of these big teams. This is so exciting! It shows that women can be amazing leaders in sports. It shows that great talent can come from anyone. This league is very important for football fans everywhere.

Why Is This So Special?

This news is super special because it's a *first*! Marie-Louise Eta is the *first* woman to be a coach for a men's team in one of Europe's top football leagues. This means it has never happened before! For a very long time, mostly men were coaches for men's teams. But now, Marie-Louise Eta is showing everyone that girls and women can do it too! She is breaking new ground. This is like when someone makes a path where there was no path before. She is showing the way for other women who love football. This is a very big step for sports and for showing that everyone can follow their dreams. It's important because it tells everyone that women are strong, smart, and can be great leaders. It doesn't matter if you are a boy or a girl. If you work hard and are good at something, you can do amazing things. This news makes the world a little bit fairer and more exciting! It's about fairness for everyone.

What Does "Interim" Mean?

When we say Marie-Louise Eta is the "interim" head coach, it means she is helping for a little while. It's like she is stepping in to help the team right now. Maybe for a few games, or until they find a new coach for a longer time. But even for a short time, this is still a huge deal! She is showing everyone what is possible. It's a very important job, even if it's just for a short time. She is making a big difference for Union Berlin. She is showing her skills right now!

Dreams Come True!

Marie-Louise Eta is an example for all of you. It shows that if you have a dream, you can work hard and make it happen. Maybe you dream of playing football. Maybe you dream of being a coach. Or maybe you dream of being a doctor, a pilot, or a teacher. Whatever your dream is, this story tells us that anything is possible. Girls can be football coaches. Boys can be great cooks. Everyone can follow their heart. It's a wonderful message for kids everywhere!

Did You Know? Fun Football Facts!

* Did you know that football is the most popular sport in the whole world? Billions of people love to play and watch football! * Did you know that Africa has many amazing football players and teams? We love football here too! Maybe one day an African woman will coach a big team! * Did you know that girls and boys can play football together? Yes, they can! * Did you know that being a good team means working together? Just like in school or with your friends! * Did you know that Germany, where Union Berlin plays, is famous for its delicious sausages and beautiful castles?

Good News for Everyone!

So, this is a happy day for football! Marie-Louise Eta is showing the world that women can be leaders in men's sports. This is good news for kids in Africa, and kids everywhere. It teaches us to believe in ourselves and to never give up on our dreams. Who knows? Maybe one day, one of you will be a famous football player or a coach, right here in Africa! Keep playing, keep learning, and keep dreaming big! That's all for today from your news reporter! Bye-bye!
summary: Marie-Louise Eta is the first woman to be a coach for a big men's football team in Europe, showing everyone that girls can lead too!
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that women can do any job, even in big sports, and inspires all kids to follow their dreams.
